---
title: "Seungju Kim"
pagetitle: "About"
about:
  id: about-bio
  template: jolla
  image: images/headshot.png
  image-title: "Seungju Kim"
  links:
    - text: "{{< iconify fluent:mail-48-filled height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: mailto:seungju7@illinois.edu
    - text: "{{< iconify academicons:google-scholar height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=JWNJV9UAAAAJ&hl
    - text: "{{< iconify la:researchgate height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Seungju-Kim-11
    - text: "{{< iconify ion:logo-linkedin height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kimseungju/
    - text: "{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0009-5166-9170
    - text: "{{< iconify octicon:mark-github-16 height=35px width=35px >}}"
      href: https://github.com/follhim
comments: false
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .edu-timeline {
          position: relative;
          padding-left: 2rem;
          margin: 1.5rem 0 2rem;
          border-left: 2px solid #FF6B35;
        }
        .edu-item {
          position: relative;
          margin-bottom: 1rem;
          display: flex;
          flex-direction: column;
          gap: 0.15rem;
        }
        .edu-item::before {
          content: '';
          position: absolute;
          left: -2.4rem;
          top: 0.4rem;
          width: 12px;
          height: 12px;
          border-radius: 50%;
          background-color: #FF6B35;
          border: 2px solid #0d1b2a;
          box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #FF6B35;
        }
        .edu-year {
          display: block;
          font-size: 0.78rem;
          font-weight: 700;
          color: #FF6B35;
          text-transform: uppercase;
          letter-spacing: 0.08em;
          margin-bottom: 0.1rem;
        }
        .edu-degree {
          display: block;
          font-size: 1.1rem;
          font-weight: 700;
          color: #ffffff;
        }
        .edu-school {
          display: block;
          font-size: 0.95rem;
          color: #a8b2be;
        }
        .edu-detail {
          display: block;
          font-size: 0.82rem;
          color: #6c757d;
          font-style: italic;
        }
        </style>
---

:::{#about-bio .text-center}
My name is Seungju Kim[^1] and thank you for visiting my personal website detailing my research interests and work!

[^1]: Click [here](https://www.name-coach.com/seungju-kim) to learn how to pronounce my name

I am a mental health researcher, [National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellow]{.highlight-term}, and <br> third-year PhD student in Clinical/Community Psychology Program at the [University of Illinois]{.highlight-term}.
:::

<div id="turnstile-container" style="display:none;margin-top:0.5rem;text-align:center;"></div>
<p id="ts-status" style="font-size:0.875rem;margin-top:0.25rem;min-height:1.2em;text-align:center;"></p>

<!-- Turnstile globals: plain script so onTurnstileLoad is reachable -->
<script>
  window._tsScriptLoaded = false;
  window._tsWidgetId     = null;
  function onTurnstileLoad() { window._tsScriptLoaded = true; }
</script>
<script src="https://challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/api.js?render=explicit&onload=onTurnstileLoad" defer></script>

<script type="module">
  const WORKER_URL = 'https://turnstile-verify.follhim.workers.dev';

  async function verifyWithWorker(token) {
    try {
      const res = await fetch(`${WORKER_URL}/verify`, {
        method:  'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body:    JSON.stringify({ token })
      });
      return await res.json();
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Worker verification error:', err);
      return { success: false };
    }
  }

  function renderWidget() {
    const container = document.getElementById('turnstile-container');
    const status    = document.getElementById('ts-status');
    if (window._tsWidgetId !== null) return;

    container.style.display = 'block';
    status.textContent = '';

    window._tsWidgetId = turnstile.render('#turnstile-container', {
      sitekey:    '0x4AAAAAADNayBUca6pR_5fG',
      execution:  'render',
      appearance: 'always',
      callback: async function(token) {
        status.textContent = 'Opening your CV…';
        const data = await verifyWithWorker(token);
        if (data.success && data.token) {
          window.location.href = `${WORKER_URL}/redeem?token=${data.token}`;
          container.style.display = 'none';
          status.textContent = '';
          turnstile.remove(window._tsWidgetId);
          window._tsWidgetId = null;
        } else {
          status.textContent = 'Verification failed — please try again.';
          turnstile.remove(window._tsWidgetId);
          window._tsWidgetId = null;
        }
      },
      'error-callback': function() {
        const status = document.getElementById('ts-status');
        status.textContent = 'Verification error — please try again.';
        turnstile.remove(window._tsWidgetId);
        window._tsWidgetId = null;
      },
      'expired-callback': function() {
        turnstile.remove(window._tsWidgetId);
        window._tsWidgetId = null;
      }
    });
  }

  // Inject CV button directly into .about-links so we own the element
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    const aboutLinks = document.querySelector('.about-links');
    if (!aboutLinks) return;

    const cvLink = document.createElement('a');
    cvLink.className = 'about-link';
    cvLink.title = 'CV';
    cvLink.innerHTML = '<span class="about-link-text"><iconify-icon icon="academicons:cv" width="35px" height="35px" inline></iconify-icon></span>';
    aboutLinks.appendChild(cvLink);

    cvLink.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
      e.preventDefault();
      const status = document.getElementById('ts-status');
      if (!window._tsScriptLoaded) {
        status.textContent = 'Still loading — please try again in a moment.';
        return;
      }
      renderWidget();
    });
  });
</script>

<div style="text-align:left;">


## Research Interests

My research is ultimately informed by my commitment to creating lasting change for the mental and behavioral health of LGBTQ+ people through three lines of research:

1.   I use [longitudinal methods]{.highlight-term} to map the dynamic pathways through which stigma, resilience, and social context accumulate over time to produce mental health disparities among LGBTQ+ individuals.

2.   I invest in the [meta-scientific infrastructure]{.highlight-term} of LGBTQ+ health research — through systematic review, meta-analysis, and creating software — to strengthen how the field measures, synthesizes, and communicates evidence.

3.  I examine how [religion and spirituality]{.highlight-term} across socioecological levels — from individual beliefs to structural policy — shape mental health for LGBTQ+ individuals, with particular attention to conservative religious contexts.

## Education

::: {.edu-timeline}

::: {.edu-item}
[2024]{.edu-year}
[B.A. in Psychology]{.edu-degree}
[Wheaton College]{.edu-school}
[Magna cum laude]{.edu-detail}
:::

::: {.edu-item}
[2026]{.edu-year}
[M.S. in Psychology]{.edu-degree}
[University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign]{.edu-school}
:::

::: {.edu-item}
[2031 (expected)]{.edu-year}
[Ph.D. in Clinical/Community Psychology]{.edu-degree}
[University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign]{.edu-school}
:::

:::

## Training

My research is grounded in a deep commitment to the [flourishing of LGBTQ+ people]{.highlight-term}. My training spans the full spectrum of religious and clinical contexts — from conservative evangelical institutions to affirming community settings — reflecting my conviction that achieving health equity for LGBTQ+ people, particularly youth in conservative families and churches, requires building bridges across differences rather than around it.

Prior to beginning my doctoral studies, I earned my B.A. in Psychology *magna cum laude* from [Wheaton College]{.highlight-term}, where I completed an honors thesis examining psychological well-being in religious mixed-orientation marriages during my junior year. My research training began as a freshman working with Dr. Sandra Rueger, before joining Dr. Mark Yarhouse at the Sexuality and Gender Identity Institute, where I contributed to a national study investigating the complex intersection of faith and LGBTQ+ identities.

Expanding my research training before senior year, I established significant external collaborations with leading researchers. At [Utah State University]{.highlight-term}, I worked with Dr. Tyler Lefevor examining the effects of religiousness and spirituality on LGBTQ+ mental health, where I developed skills in psychometric scale development and validation using R's {psych} and {lavaan} packages.

Simultaneously, I joined Dr. David Wang at [Fuller Theological Seminary]{.highlight-term}, analyzing longitudinal data from a $1.9 million study on spiritual formation. This collaboration strengthened my experience in sophisticated longitudinal modeling using R and Mplus, examining complex patterns of change over time.

My quantitative methodological expertise was further enhanced when I was selected for the competitive [NSF-REU Health Equity](https://mhdi.unl.edu/undergraduate-research-programs/nsf-reu-site-community-engaged-training-advancing-health-equity/) summer internship at the [University of Nebraska-Lincoln]{.highlight-term}. Working with Drs. Katie Edwards and Lorey Wheeler on multiple NIH-funded studies examining LGBTQ+ youth and emerging adults, I gained advanced skills for identifying mischievous responders in survey data, regression models, and scale development. I was later recruited as a post-bac research associate at the [UNL methodology and psychometrics center](https://mapacademy.unl.edu/).

Before entering graduate school, I was awarded the [National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship](https://www.nsfgrfp.org/), among one of the only awardees at Wheaton.

</div>
